---
title: "Entry title goes here"
subtitle: "One-line subtitle — what this entry is really about"
date: YYYY-MM-DD
categories: [tag1, tag2, tag3]
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Source(s):** Author, *Title*. Publisher, year. [Link](#).

If this entry is built around an external resource, name it up front. The summary below is in **my own words** — what I took away. Read the original for the actual material.
:::

## Concept

What is this thing, in plain language? One or two paragraphs. Resist the urge to copy definitions — write it as if you're explaining it to a colleague over coffee.

## Why it matters

Why does this concept earn space in my head? Tie it to something concrete — a project I'm working on, a problem I've hit, a decision it would change.

## Code: a worked example

```{r}
#| eval: false
# A small, runnable example that shows the concept in action.
# Use realistic shapes (PK data, MEPS-style records) when possible.
```

Walk through what the code does and why, in one or two sentences after.

## Resources used

- **Primary resource:** [link]. One sentence on what it is and how I used it.
- **Supporting resource:** [link]. One sentence.
- *(Cheat sheets, vignettes, blog posts that helped.)*

## My summary — key concepts that helped me understand

- **Bolded concept name.** One or two sentences explaining it in my own words.
- **Another concept.** Same pattern.
- *(3–7 bullets is the sweet spot. If I'm writing more, I'm probably copying instead of summarizing.)*

## Gotchas / things to remember

::: {.callout-warning}
The thing I most want future-me to know before I touch this again.
:::

- Specific things that tripped me up.
- Function name collisions, default behavior surprises, edge cases.
- "This only works when…" caveats.

## Status

In progress / Reviewed / Re-read / Applied to [project]. What's next.

## Cross-references

- *Related entries:* [link to another entry in this site].
- *Resources Reviewed:* [link to the entry in the resources index].
